title: Non-folding transforms preserve source-row identity
summary: Only folding may change which source rows are displayed; every other frontend transform preserves one displayed row per source row.
details: Line numbers must remain stable while navigating, debugging, stepping, and reading diagnostics.
A non-folding transform may blank a source row or radically rewrite content within it, but it may not merge that row into another displayed row or synthesize a different row mapping.
The invariant concerns vertical/source-row identity. Horizontal presentation inside one row is intentionally unconstrained.
rationale: Debugger and diagnostic locations are expressed in source lines, so changing displayed row identity outside an explicit fold makes the frontend operationally misleading.

provenance.note: Owner declared this a hard rule and requested it be persisted for all future agents.

evidence.claim: Interaction coverage asserts that reveal transitions do not change source-buffer lines.

aliases: one displayed row per source row
questions: May a frontend feature combine two source lines?
Why must line numbers remain unchanged while stepping?
Which feature is allowed to collapse displayed rows?

last_verified.method: Owner statement checked against current conceal and virtual-text implementation.
